response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned senior engineer with genuine geospatial experience (GISCollective, Green Map, FOSS4G speaker) and real low-latency distributed-systems work handling millions of requests per second in DLang. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ or Rust, and his C++ exposure is brief and dated (a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a thesis project), with no Rust at all. His recent work at Google and Meta is full-stack TypeScript/Node product engineering, and he has no direct navigation guidance or routing-pipeline experience, making him an adjacent rather than direct fit.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS domain experience through GISCollective, Green Map, and a FOSS4G talk
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) on microservices handling millions of requests per second
Broad senior-level engineering track record with strong Linux, CI/CD, and testing craftsmanship
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; no Rust experience
No navigation guidance, routing, or map-tile pipeline experience specifically
Recent roles center on full-stack TypeScript web development, far from systems-level data pipeline work
